commonSteps.title: Verify the evidence chain
commonSteps.description: Require SQLite integrity, both append-only triggers, and the complete HMAC chain before relying on this incident evidence.

commonSteps.title: Confirm current incident state
commonSteps.description: Check whether the authoritative SLO or desired-state source has emitted a retained resolution for this incident generation.

commonSteps.title: Review preceding change candidates
commonSteps.description: Inspect every bounded temporal and scope correlation without treating score or ordering as proof of root cause.

commonSteps.title: Review response evidence
commonSteps.description: Inspect bounded events after incident onset for recovery attempts, verification, recurrence, and the final authoritative resolution.

steps.title: Run bounded stack diagnosis
steps.description: Use the native stack-status diagnostic to inspect PostgreSQL container state, bounded database readiness, routing, and dependent services.

steps.title: Review bounded PostgreSQL and service logs
steps.description: Correlate database and dependent service logs with incident onset while retaining redaction and output limits.

steps.title: Use guarded stateful service recovery only if justified
steps.description: If diagnosis proves a lifecycle fault, use project-confined service control so normal health and post-start hooks remain authoritative.

steps.title: Run bounded stack diagnosis
steps.description: Inspect Director, Gateway, TextRouter, PostgreSQL, and RabbitMQ state through the native stack-status diagnostic.

steps.title: Run bounded RabbitMQ diagnosis
steps.description: Probe both configured RabbitMQ services and compare their project-container state without sending or deleting messages.

steps.title: Recover only the proven failed service
steps.description: Use repository service control for the exact failed component; stateful services retain their independent second gate.

steps.title: Inspect required-map readiness
steps.description: Use stack-status to compare configured always-on policy, container state, farm registration, and readiness evidence.

steps.title: Review the affected map and routing logs
steps.description: Identify whether the failure is container lifecycle, farm registration, routing, database, or post-start patch related before recovery.

steps.title: Use post-hook-aware map recovery
steps.description: Recover the exact required map through repository service control so bridge, health, Coriolis validation, and runtime patches are restored.

steps.title: Review backup age and verification
steps.description: Inspect the newest recognized dump, manifest, world identity, archive layers, and prior verification outcome before creating more evidence.

steps.title: Run bounded storage diagnosis
steps.description: Check workspace, backup, and container storage capacity without cleanup or deletion.

steps.title: Create and verify a full backup
steps.description: After storage and source health are confirmed, create a new layered backup and require the normal full verifier to pass.

steps.title: Review the last restore-drill receipt
steps.description: Inspect source digest, disposable restore result, Dune invariants, RPO/RTO, cleanup, and receipt-chain integrity.

steps.title: Run bounded storage diagnosis
steps.description: Confirm enough local capacity exists for a no-network disposable restore and round-trip dump.

steps.title: Run an isolated restore rehearsal
steps.description: Queue the hardened no-network restore drill against a reviewed backup; this never writes the live game database.

steps.title: Inspect host and map memory pressure
steps.description: Use stack-status and retained capacity evidence to identify the actual consumers and current map-demand state.

steps.title: Review evidence-qualified capacity recommendations
steps.description: Compare warm-idle cost, revisits, request-to-ready latency, confidence, memory floor, and current operator-selected modes.

steps.title: Apply only eligible gradual recommendations
steps.description: Use the existing evidence gate and bounded application fraction; never change always-on or explicitly disabled map modes.

steps.title: Review credential and identity posture
steps.description: Confirm token enforcement, a non-placeholder recovery credential, RBAC file validity, federated mapping, allowed hosts, and allowed origins.

steps.title: Review bounded authentication events
steps.description: Inspect failed, throttled, and successful authentication metadata without exposing submitted credentials or session secrets.

steps.title: Repair configuration through guarded settings
steps.description: Change only the proven invalid access setting, retain the owner recovery path, and verify login before ending the existing session.

steps.title: Review every open drift finding
steps.description: Compare file digests and container fields with the approved baseline; acknowledgement records ownership but cannot resolve drift.

steps.title: Verify deployment provenance
steps.description: Match changed repository files to the intended published commit and separately review environment, secret-file, mount, image, and container changes.

steps.title: Revert drift or seal the fully reviewed state
steps.description: Prefer restoring the approved source when drift is unintended; seal only after every current finding is reviewed because sealing approves the complete snapshot.

steps.title: Preserve the current ledger before intervention
steps.description: Do not delete rows, remove triggers, rotate the key, or overwrite the database; preserve it for comparison and forensic review.

steps.title: Verify the newest matching backup set
steps.description: Use the backup-bound policy, key, and SQLite snapshot to identify the newest independently valid event chain.

steps.title: Restore only from a matching verified backup
steps.description: Use the guarded restore preflight and preserve the suspect ledger separately; a newly generated key cannot repair historical signatures.

steps.title: Run bounded stack diagnosis
steps.description: Establish current service, database, routing, and resource state before selecting a narrower recovery path.

steps.title: Review objective context and logs
steps.description: Use the objective snapshot, retained logs, candidates, and follow-up events to define the failed contract before intervention.

steps.title: Review the exact drift finding
steps.description: Inspect category, subject, changed fields, criticality, first/last observation, and any ownership note without exposing secret values.

steps.title: Compare with intended source and deployment evidence
steps.description: Verify repository checksums and independently explain environment, key, image, mount, and runtime fingerprint changes.

steps.title: Revert unintended drift or seal reviewed state
steps.description: Use the normal deployment/rollback path for unintended change; seal only when the complete observed snapshot is the intended approved state.

steps.title: Define the failed contract from evidence
steps.description: Review the incident event, scope, candidates, and follow-up evidence before choosing any recovery workflow.

steps.title: Run bounded stack diagnosis when applicable
steps.description: Use the native read-only stack diagnostic to establish current runtime state without executing shell text or accepting arguments.
